entities.name: RUNOFF
entities.description: The flow of water over the land surface during rainfall events.

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The removal of soil from the land surface due to runoff.

entities.name: SEDIMENT CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The amount of sediment particles present in the runoff water.

entities.name: PARTICLE SIZE DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The relative proportions of different grain sizes in the eroded sediment.

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: Clay-rich soils characterized by shrinking and swelling properties.

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTER, PATANCHERU, INDIA
entities.description: The geographical site where the agricultural watersheds were studied.

entities.name: STORM SIZE
entities.description: The magnitude and intensity of rainfall events.

entities.name: LAND MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
entities.description: Agricultural practices used to manage land and mitigate soil erosion.

entities.name: CLAY CONTENT
entities.description: The proportion of clay-sized particles within the eroded material or top soil.

entities.name: TOP SOIL
entities.description: The uppermost layer of the soil profile in the watersheds.

relationships.description: Runoff events cause the erosion and removal of soil.
Runoff rates influence the peak concentration of sediment.
The process of runoff affects the size distribution of eroded sediment.
The size of the storm affects the total amount of soil lost.
Storm size influences the size distribution of the eroded sediment.
Land management systems significantly affect the amount of soil loss.
Land management systems have a significant effect on runoff.
Land management treatments influence the particle size distribution of eroded materials.
Smaller storms generally produce eroded material with higher clay content than larger storms.
Runoff events were measured on watersheds consisting of Vertisols.
The study of Vertisols was conducted at the ICRISAT Center in Patancheru, India.
